$description: Machine-readable frontend/backend parity contract ledger for Passage Zero. Enforced by scripts/check-frontend-backend-parity.js (pnpm test:parity). Do not hand-edit status to 'implemented' or set frontend.user_visible=true without a real, existing route/component -- the checker verifies file existence, not intent.
owner_scope_note: Maintained by the frontend/backend parity enforcement lane only. Does not modify app/, components/, lib/auth/, lib/supabase/, supabase/, vercel.json, or scripts/vercel-ignore-build.js -- those are owned by the Cycle 7A invitation UI / deployment-gate lane.

statuses.implemented: A reachable frontend route/component AND a real backend server command/durable table/RLS predicate/failure states are both wired together and evidenced.
statuses.backend_only: The backend server command, durable tables, RLS predicate, and (if applicable) append-only event exist and are evidenced, but no frontend route or component currently calls it. frontend.user_visible must be false.
statuses.queued: Neither side is built yet, or only partial groundwork exists. Only the planned target is documented. Must not claim a reachable UI (frontend.user_visible must be false, route/component must be null).

contracts.capability: Director/owner creates a staff invitation scoped to an organization and one or more locations

frontend.note: The form is reachable only for an authenticated owner/director on the exact isolated greenfield Preview route. Both the route boundary and Server Action re-check isolated-project configuration, active organization membership, role, and location authority before invoking the command.

contracts.action_status: ready -> submitting -> pending_acceptance receipt. A first command returns a one-time raw invitation token plus the durable invitation id and persisted created_at; same-key replay returns the same id/timestamp with no raw token or duplicate rows/events.
contracts.server_command: public.create_employee_invitation_idempotent_v2(p_organization_id uuid, p_invited_email text, p_organization_location_ids uuid[], p_purpose text, p_expires_at timestamptz, p_idempotency_key text) -> passage_private.create_employee_invitation_idempotent_v2

durable_tables.cardinality: one row per invitation; unique token_digest; terminal-state check prevents accepted+revoked simultaneously
one row per (invitation, location); composite primary key

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: passage_private.can_manage_invitation(id) -> passage_private.can_manage_organization (active membership, role in owner/director) AND passage_private.can_manage_location for every location on the invitation (active grant to an owner/director member)
failure_recovery_states: route-disabled outside the exact isolated Preview runtime, on the wrong project ref, or when password Preview auth is disabled
same-user retry uses the same idempotency key and returns the original durable id/created_at without exposing the one-time token again
already-active-member (Postgres 23505: 'This person is already an active organization member')
missing active organization membership / actor authority (Postgres 42501)
environment-unavailable (Supabase client cannot be constructed, e.g. missing env vars)
contracts.persona_projection: Visible only to the inviting owner/director's organization; invited_email and purpose are never exposed to family, staff, or vendor personas. The select policy restricts rows to invited_by_user_id, accepted_by_user_id, or passage_private.can_manage_invitation(id).

contracts.capability: Invited staff member inspects an invitation (inviter, organization, role, locations, purpose, expiry, state) before signing in

frontend.note: Server component renders the inspection result via public.inspect_organization_invitation before any sign-in is required, and fails closed on invalid/malformed tokens (validInvitationToken).

contracts.action_status: invitation_state resolves to one of available | accepted | revoked | expired on every page load

durable_tables.cardinality: one row per invitation, looked up by sha256 token_digest

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: public.inspect_organization_invitation is SECURITY INVOKER over a SECURITY DEFINER passage_private core, granted to anon+authenticated; the private schema itself is not exposed by the Data API. The token digest match is the sole scoping predicate (a session/role is not required pre-sign-in by design).

append_only_event.reason: Inspection is a read-only lookup by token digest. The Cycle 7A migration does not emit a workflow_events row for inspection, only for created/accepted/revoked. No durable proof of 'who viewed this invitation and when' currently exists.

failure_recovery_states: invalid token shape -> 'This invitation link is incomplete or invalid.' (client-side validInvitationToken gate, no RPC call made)
environment-unavailable -> configuration.reason surfaced, RPC not attempted
RPC error -> generic retry failure message, no partial state shown
invitation_state = expired | revoked | accepted -> distinct calm copy per state, no acceptance form rendered
contracts.persona_projection: Shows only the fields the invitation itself carries (inviter name, org name, role, location names, purpose, expiry, state); never exposes other pending invitations, roster, or case data.

contracts.capability: Invited staff member accepts an invitation, creating or updating their organization membership and location grants

frontend.note: acceptInvitation is a deliberate POST Server Action bound to a <form>; a GET receipt view may only replay an already-accepted invitation, never perform the first acceptance.

contracts.action_status: pending_acceptance -> accepted (idempotent: a same-user replay RPC call returns the same receipt with replayed=true and no duplicate rows)

durable_tables.cardinality: one row created or updated per accepted invitation; unique (organization_id, user_id) where status='active'
one row per (member, granted location); composite primary key with revoked_at upsert on replay

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: SECURITY DEFINER RPC requires auth.uid() IS NOT NULL and a verified (email_confirmed_at) email matching invitation.invited_email; the subsequent organization_member_locations select is scoped by passage_private.can_view_member_location.
failure_recovery_states: email-mismatch (42501) when the verified session email differs from the invitation's invited_email
claimed-other (23505/42501) when a matching membership is already bound to another account or needs administrator review
unavailable (22023) for invalid, expired, revoked, or already-claimed-by-a-different-user tokens
verification failure -> access remains closed if the replay RPC does not return the same organization_member_id/organization_id/member_role as the first call
previously-fixed 42702 ON CONFLICT ambiguity (migration 20260716130000) that had rolled back every first-time acceptance before the corrective migration
contracts.persona_projection: Only the accepting authenticated user's own resulting membership/receipt is returned; no other organization member, family, or vendor persona can observe an in-flight acceptance.

contracts.capability: Staff lands on /staff after sign-in or acceptance, gated by a durable, RLS-backed authority check

frontend.note: OperationalBoundary resolves the viewer server-side on every request (dynamic='force-dynamic', revalidate=0) and fails closed to 'Workspace access remains closed' rather than rendering staff content when authority is unavailable. IMPORTANT: the content rendered once the gate opens is still lib/sandbox/repository fixture data, not durable Supabase-backed assigned work -- see cycle7b.staff.assigned_work below for that gap.

contracts.action_status: auth-gate resolved per request: environment-unavailable | signed-out | membership-required | membership-selection-required | authority-unavailable | ok

durable_tables.cardinality: exactly one active row expected per (organization,user); more than one triggers membership-selection-required fail-closed
zero or more active (revoked_at is null) grants per member

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: organization_member_locations select is scoped by passage_private.can_view_member_location(organization_member_id, organization_location_id): either the row's own member (auth.uid()) or a manager with location authority.

append_only_event.reason: Landing is a read-authorized page render, not a state mutation; no workflow_events row is emitted for a successful (or denied) landing view. See cycle7b.command_audit for the queued gap this creates.

failure_recovery_states: environment-unavailable -> 'Workspace access remains closed', no Supabase client available
signed-out -> redirect to /login with a return path
membership-required -> fail closed, no membership row
membership-selection-required -> fail closed pending disambiguation (more than one active membership)
authority-unavailable -> fail closed (unrecognized role or zero active location grants)
contracts.persona_projection: Only the signed-in user's own membership and location grants are read; no roster of other staff or organizations is exposed on this route.

contracts.capability: A staff-role member is denied access to /director (and symmetrically, an owner/director-role member is denied /staff)

frontend.note: A signed-in staff-role member hitting /director is fail-closed by canOpenOperationalPath before any director-only content renders; post-deploy browser proof recorded '/director' returning 'Workspace access remains closed' for the wrong role.

contracts.server_command: lib/auth/authorization.ts#canOpenOperationalPath(role, pathname), evaluated inside components/auth/OperationalBoundary.tsx during server render

durable_tables.cardinality: role column (owner|director|staff) read once per request to drive the denial decision

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: Role separation here is an application-layer check (canOpenOperationalPath), not a Postgres RLS policy -- /director currently renders sandbox-fixture content rather than a real per-row Supabase query. The underlying membership read that feeds the decision is itself RLS-scoped to the caller's own row.

append_only_event.reason: No workflow_events row is emitted for a denied cross-role navigation attempt today. This is a known observability gap this ledger surfaces for cycle7b.command_audit to close.

failure_recovery_states: staff role at /director -> fail closed, no case/workload data fetched
owner/director role at /staff -> fail closed (symmetric)
the same environment-unavailable/signed-out/membership fail-closed states as cycle7a.staff.landing apply before the role check even runs
contracts.persona_projection: A denied viewer sees only a generic closed-access message; no case, workload, or roster data leaks across the role boundary.

contracts.capability: Staff's /staff landing shows durable, RLS-scoped assigned tasks/workflows instead of client-side sandbox fixtures

frontend.note: The existing /staff route (app/staff/page.tsx) renders lib/sandbox/repository fixture data today, not durable assigned work. This queued contract tracks replacing that with a real Supabase-backed, RLS-scoped query. Until it lands, /staff must not be cited as proof of durable assigned-work.

contracts.action_status: planned: tasks.assigned_organization_member_id already exists as a column (added by the Cycle 7A migration) but is not yet populated or queried by any route
contracts.server_command: planned: an RLS-scoped select (or wrapping RPC) against public.tasks/public.workflows filtered by assigned_organization_member_id = passage_private.current_active_member_id(organization_id)

durable_tables.cardinality: planned -- column tasks.assigned_organization_member_id exists (Cycle 7A migration) but is unused by any query today
planned -- assignment/state-change events for tasks are not yet emitted

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: planned: an RLS policy scoping public.tasks/public.workflows select to the caller's active organization_member_id and granted organization_location_id, mirroring passage_private.can_manage_location

append_only_event.reason: Not yet implemented; no assignment event is emitted today.

failure_recovery_states: planned: reconnect/replay must preserve assignment truth, per the operational-readiness reconnect gate
planned: RLS must deny cross-organization or cross-location task reads
contracts.persona_projection: planned: staff sees only tasks where assigned_organization_member_id matches their own active membership id

contracts.capability: Director reassigns a commitment/task to another staff member with durable RLS authority and an audit trail, replacing the in-memory sandbox reassignment dispatch

frontend.note: app/director/page.tsx already has a reassignment form, but it dispatches to the client-side sandbox reducer (lib/sandbox/repository), not a durable/RLS-backed server command; state is lost on reload. This queued contract tracks the durable replacement.

contracts.action_status: planned: assigned -> reassigned, with previous_state/next_state and a required reason recorded on the audit event
contracts.server_command: planned: e.g. public.reassign_task(p_task_id uuid, p_new_assignee_organization_member_id uuid, p_reason text) with a passage_private.can_manage_location authority check

durable_tables.cardinality: planned -- update assigned_organization_member_id guarded by an authority + location-membership check
planned -- one append-only row per reassignment with previous_state/next_state

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: planned: only owner/director members with an active grant on the task's organization_location_id may update assigned_organization_member_id

append_only_event.reason: Not yet implemented; the current sandbox dispatch is in-memory only and lost on reload, so no durable event exists to audit.

failure_recovery_states: planned: reassignment to a member without an active location grant must be rejected
planned: reason is required and stored on the audit event, mirroring the existing invitation-revocation reason requirement
contracts.persona_projection: planned: director/owner scoped to their own organization/location authority only; staff cannot reassign

contracts.capability: Director/owner revokes a pending invitation through a director-facing UI with a visible pending-invitation list and audit surfacing

frontend.note: The passage_private.revoke_organization_invitation / public.revoke_organization_invitation RPC and its append-only event already exist from the Cycle 7A migration and are independently reachable via direct RPC call, but there is no director-facing UI to list pending invitations or trigger a revocation, and no surfaced audit view. The full user-facing capability (list + trigger + surfaced result) is queued for Cycle 7B; the backend groundwork is not.

contracts.action_status: backend already implemented: pending_acceptance -> revoked (idempotent replay returns the same receipt); frontend not yet built
contracts.server_command: public.revoke_organization_invitation(p_invitation_id uuid, p_reason text) -> passage_private.revoke_organization_invitation (RPC already implemented; UI wiring is what is queued)

durable_tables.cardinality: already implemented (Cycle 7A) -- revoked_at/revoked_by_user_id/revocation_reason columns with a shape check constraint

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: already implemented: passage_private.can_manage_invitation(id) gate inside the RPC; no direct table UPDATE grant exists for authenticated (writes only via the SECURITY DEFINER function)
failure_recovery_states: already implemented: accepted invitations reject revocation (55000) and require the separate membership-revocation workflow instead
already implemented: replay on an already-revoked invitation returns the same receipt (replayed=true) without a duplicate event
planned: a director-facing UI must surface these states without requiring a direct RPC call
contracts.persona_projection: planned: pending-invitation list scoped to organization/location authority, matching the create_invitation projection

contracts.capability: Director-facing, append-only command/audit trail (who did what, when, to what, with what proof) per the persona-action-architecture's universal action contract

frontend.note: docs/product/persona-action-architecture.md specifies /director/cases/[id]/timeline and /organization/audit as target surfaces; neither exists in app/ yet. workflow_events rows already exist (invitation created/accepted/revoked) but are not queried or rendered anywhere in the product.

contracts.action_status: planned: read-only projection of the existing workflow_events stream, scoped by manage-authority
contracts.server_command: planned: an RLS-scoped select (or wrapping RPC) against public.workflow_events filtered by organization_id/organization_location_id authority

durable_tables.cardinality: rows already exist for invitation create/accept/revoke (Cycle 7A); no RLS policy or route exposes them to any persona yet

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: planned: the Cycle 7A migration explicitly does not enforce an append-only/RLS cutover for workflow_events general reads; a director/owner-scoped audit-read policy remains to be written

append_only_event.event_type: n/a -- this contract is about reading the existing event stream, not writing a new event type
append_only_event.reason: Read-surface contract; no new write event is introduced by this capability.

failure_recovery_states: planned: audit reads must fail closed for any organization/location the caller does not manage
planned: export/evidence generation (persona-action-architecture's /organization/audit) is out of scope until read access itself is scoped
contracts.persona_projection: planned: owner/director see their organization's/location's events only; no persona-agnostic global audit view
